observations.recommendation:
1.4	We recommended that Management direct:
The Municipal Assessor to prepare and furnish the Municipal Treasurer the assessment rolls of real properties for the computation real property taxes and updating of RPTAR/RPTIC;
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due for current and delinquent real property taxes based on RPTAR/RPTIC; and
The Municipal Accountant to:
Establish the amount of RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year based on the computation provided by the Acting Municipal Treasurer;
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer relative to the balance of delinquent RPT/SET receivables and draw the necessary journal entries to reconcile the balances of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts; and
Prepare subsidiary ledgers for the delinquent RPT and SET receivables.

2.7	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Request for write-off of dormant accounts that can no longer be accounted or settled following the prescribed guidelines and procedures in reconciling and cleaning the books of accounts;
Maintain complete subsidiary ledger and individual ledger cards for each debtor in each receivable account on active status indicating therein the complete details of the transaction to avoid additional inclusion of accounts with unreliable existence in the financial statements; and
Prepare a quarterly monitoring report with aging and issue demand letters when necessary to ensure that these are collected when these become due.

3.5	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation costs on the aforecited PPE items and draw the necessary journal entries to take up the corresponding depreciation expenses, prior years’ adjustments and accumulated depreciation to present a reliable and accurate PPE account balance in the financial statements.

4.7	We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Record the proposed adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and
Ensure adherence to the recognition criteria for PPE accounts.

5.6	We recommended that Management, together with the members of the GAD Focal Point System, ensure that PPAs incorporated in the GPB fully address gender issues and concerns and align with the purpose of the GAD objectives of the LGU.

6.6	We recommended that Management require the Local School Board to appropriate and utilize the SEF in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations and discontinue the incurrence of expenditures which are not related thereto.

7.11	We recommended that Management instruct the BAC together with the Technical Working Group to conduct thorough verification of the Class “A” Eligibility Documents listed in Annex “A” of the PhilGEPS Certificate of Registration and Membership and guarantee that contracts are awarded only to eligible bidders.
7.12	We further recommended that Management direct the BAC to refrain from recommending award of contract to bidders who do not possess the required license to transact business.
8.6	We recommended that Management devise or formulate policy guidelines on the grant of donations in kind to ensure regularity of transactions and proper utilization of government funds.

9.6	We recommended that Management:
Exhaust possible means to ensure that utilization of the Mobile Medical Clinic with X-ray is maximized to prevent wear and tear due to exposure to elements, obsolescence or non-usage; and
Properly plan and assess the procurement of equipment in terms of availability of knowledgeable personnel qualified to operate it and immediate need of the same to obtain the maximum use of the equipment.

10.17	We recommended that Management:
Direct the Business Permit and Licensing Officer/Municipal Treasurer to:
Examine the accuracy of the declared gross receipts/sales of suppliers and contractors;
Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and obtain information or summary of the transactions of suppliers/contractors doing business within the LGU; and
Strictly enforce the procedures in the imposition of business taxes set forth in the Municipal Revenue Code, especially the submission of sworn statement of gross receipts or sales and certified copy of Income Tax Returns to accurately assess the business taxes.
Coordinate with the SB to prioritize the revision of the Municipal Revenue Code that will enhance the revenue-raising potential of the LGU to realize more funds to support PPAs beneficial to the constituents.

a)	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require all collectors to remit in full all their collections observing proper cut-off procedures in their remittance of collections, and;

•	See to it that all collectors are properly supervised and their accountabilities are regularly monitored; and

•	Deposit intact daily or not later than the next banking day all their collections as well as collections turned over to them by the collectors to the AGDB.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to submit copy of the General Journals for CY 2019, original copy of
Journal entry vouchers for amounts posted in the general journal and in the
general ledger and its supporting documents for validation of the
transactions/amounts referred therein.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the journal entry vouchers together with the supporting
documents listed therein for the audit team to validate the propriety of the PPE
which were dropped from the books, evaluate the reliability of the procedures
adopted in the disposal of unserviceable properties and transfers of assets to
other LGUs/barangays made to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Assessor to
exhaust all possible means for speedy titling of land and acquire Certificate of
Title in the name of the Municipality in order to protect the interest of the
Municipality for any adverse claims by third parties and in compliance with the
above-cited laws, rules and regulation.

We recommended that Management:
1. Require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the updating of the
assessment roll, a copy of which is to be furnished to the Municipal
Treasurer to serve as basis in updating the RPTAR or taxpayers index
cards to arrive at a correct amount of real property tax receivables;
and
2. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal
Accountant a copy of the certified list showing the name of taxpayers
and the amount due and collectible every year to be used as basis in
setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the actual collectibles
from real property tax in the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
1. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reverse the Accounting Journal
Entries made in writing-off the afore-mentioned Accounts Receivable
and submit explanation on the journal entries made affecting the
Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable accounts;
2. Submit the list of schedules of Accounts Payable account amounting
to P5,476,780.26 including the schedules of said account as of
December 31, 20019; and
3. Strictly adhere to the existing rules and regulations in writing-off
Accounts Receivable and reversion of Accounts Payable to
Government Equity.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
make a journal entry voucher to record the shortage incurred by the Municipal
Treasurer in the 2017 Cash Examination as certified by the Audit Team who
conducted the cash examination pursuant to the Revised Cash Examination
Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to review all payments made by BMEMPC and the corresponding
journal entries made to record said payments and to reconcile with the records
of BMEMPC and draw adjusting entries to correct the erroneous journal entries
made, if necessary, and furnish copy of the same to the audit team for
verification.

Further, we recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant to submit a copy of the loan agreement/MOA for the loan granted
by the LGU to BMEMPC to support the disbursement of P879,000.00 and a
copy of MOA designating the LGU as trustee for the receipt of P745,020.30
from BMEMPC including the list of employees with the corresponding amount
to whom said transfer of fund to LGU was held as trustee to support the journal
entry made in the cash receipts journal under Official Receipt No. 4004197,
dated February 11, 2019.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of paying loans of
employees that is more than the amount withheld and to remit to lending
institutions the actual amount withheld from the salaries of the Municipal
employees.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to submit a written justification relative to the
remittances made for loan amortization of employees which were more than the
amount withheld from their salaries and utilization of funds from PhilHealth
Care were used for other purposes for evaluation of audit team.

We recommended that the Management:
1. Strictly adhere to the existing rules and regulations in the grant of
honoraria to BAC members and TWG;
2. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge expenses of BAC
Honorarium in accordance with the provisions of DBM Budget
Circular No. 207-3 dated November 7, 2007.
3. Require the Municipal Accountant to treat the proceeds from the sale
of bid documents as trust receipts and direct the Municipal
Accountant to record the transaction using the proper accounts as
prescribed in COA Circular 2015-009; and
4. Require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to
submit an explanation why the BAC Honoraria was charged to the
Approved Annual Budget under Other Professional Benefits, and
require the BAC members and TWG to refund the amount charged
thereto pursuant to the aforementioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive limit the hiring of experts to its
actual need of consultancy services, taking into consideration the specific work
requiring highly specialized or technical expertise in a field of special knowledge
or training that cannot be done by the regular staff of the Municipality.

We likewise recommended that the aforementioned documentary
requirements be submitted by the consultants for further evaluation of the Audit
Team.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to exercise due
diligence in the issuance of checks to avoid penalty. We likewise recommended
that the remaining bank charges (penalty) amounting to 13,400.00 should be
charged against the Municipal Treasurer. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant
to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the previous journal entry made by
recognizing a receivable account to be charged to the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of the
afore-quoted Circular in the payment of operating expenses and stop the
granting of cash advances to municipal officials and employees who are not
designated as disbursing officer, except for traveling expenses in attending
seminars.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the fund transfers received
from national government agencies to the trust fund books and maintain
subsidiary ledgers thereto. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to submit
justification regarding the incurred expenditures which were not related to the
purpose or intention of the funds received but were charged to said funds and
made the necessary adjustment in the books. Further, we also recommended
that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit a
justification together with the supporting documents relative to JEV No. 2019-
12-122 on adjustments which affected various accounts, for the evaluation of the
Audit Team.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer
and Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate bank account under Trust
Fund and to transfer the unexpended balances of QRF and Mitigation Fund -
MOOE to the Trust Fund books for easy monitoring of the utilization of
LDRRMF and ensure availability of the fund to support disaster risk and
reduction and management activities of LDRRMC.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds, and Subsidy
from Other Funds and Subsidy to Other Funds, to determine the cause/s of the
inconsistencies and effect the necessary adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the
affected accounts into agreement.

We recommended that Management advise the Bids and Awards
Committee to post in the PhilGEPS website the Request for Quotation/Request
for Proposal of each procurement undertaken through alternative methods in
order to obtain better price quotations from wider range of suppliers. We also
recommended that the Management require the BAC Secretariat to post in the
PhilGEPS website the Notice of Award, Contract and Notice to Proceed
pursuant to the above-quoted laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management adhere to the afore-quoted
provisions of the Local Treasury Operations Manual and MNGAS in the
preparation and releasing of checks to attain stronger internal control.

We recommended that Management:
1. Continue appropriating GAD Funds of not less than 5% of the total
budget and submit the GAD Plan and Budget for review of the DILG
on time;
2. Execute those plans accordingly in order to achieve the desired
results;
3. Instruct the lead person/responsible office to implement the GAD
Plan accordingly in order to fully achieve the performance target
indicated in the GAD Plan; and
4. Maximize the utilization of GAD Budget to pursue womens
empowerment and gender equality.

We recommended that Management formulate and immediately
implement a long-term solution leading to a self-reliant LGU, as mandated by the
Local Government Code, to address the disposal of its solid wastes.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer
and other officials concerned to exert concerted efforts to collect the delinquent
real property taxes; otherwise, apply the remedies provided under the law in
order to generate more revenue that may be appropriated for the
implementation of additional programs and projects for the benefit of its
constituents and enforce the sanctions provided under the law for non-
compliance therewith.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to submit a valid explanation and supporting documents to justify
the journal entries made in reclassifying the payables to the BIR to Accounts
Payable account. We likewise recommended that Management require the
Municipal Accountant to be judicious in making adjusting journal entries.
Further, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to draw the
necessary journal entries to revert the adjusting entries which were not duly
supported with documentation.

We recommended that
Management strictly follow the
provisions of Section 3a of
Revenue Regulations No. 10-
2008 and Section 2 of Revenue
Regulations No. 8-2018 in
withholding of taxes on all
compensations including
honoraria being received by the
officials and employees of the
Municipality, and remit the same
to the BIR in accordance with the
said regulations.

We recommended that the
Municipal Accountant ensure the
submission of monthly report of
financial transactions, vouchers
and payrolls, collection accounts
together with the official receipts
and deposit slips within the
reglementary period in
compliance with the
aforementioned COA Circulars
and institute other measures as
maybe necessary.

We recommended that
Management stop the practice of
charging expenditures against the
LDRRMF which are not directly
supportive in the implementation
of disaster risk management
programs and activities. We
further recommended that
Management allocate a separate
budget under the General Fund
for MOOE of the MDRRMO
pursuant to the above-cited
Circular.

We recommended that
Management advise the
approving officer to stop
approving loan applications of
employees, the amortization of
which may result in a net take
home pay of less than the
threshold, in compliance with the
above-cited law.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal  Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every year to be used as basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the actual collectibles from real property tax in the financial statements of the Municipality.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The restrictions given by the Management may be considered superficial for non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on setting-up of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax receivable based on RPTAR. The said cases may be isolated and immaterial as regards the whole population concerned.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurerto deposit immediately the balance of cash on hand amounting to ₱4,499,266.85 with the authorized depository account of the Municipality, and comply with the proper handling and recording of collections and deposits in accordance with the Local Treasury Operations Manual to ensure prompt reporting, remittances and deposit of collections; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the regular deposit of collection pursuant to the above -cited rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management strictly follow the provisions of Section 3a of Revenue Regulations No. 10-2008 and Section 2 of Revenue Regulations No. 8-2018 in withholding of taxes on all compensations including honoraria being received by the officials and employees of the Municipality, and remit the same to the BIR in accordance with the  said regulations.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team is yet to verify the management’s comment pending submission of the documents up to this writing to substantiate their claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the submission of monthly report of financial transactions, vouchers and payrolls, collection accounts together with the official receipts and deposit slips within the reglementary period in compliance with the aforementioned COA Circulars and institute other measures as maybe necessary.

We recommended that Management STOP the practice of charging expenditures against the LDRRMF which are not directly supportive in the implementation of disaster risk management programs and activities.
Further, we recommended that management allocate a separate budget under the General Fund for MOOE of the MDRRMO pursuant to the above-cited Circular.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The general and administrative expenses such as repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles used by the MDRRMO may not be considered as falling under the disaster preparedness but may be classified as MOOE pursuant to Section 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, dated April 4, 2014, hence we maintain our audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management fill up the mandatory positions in the LDRRMO in accordance with the above-quoted Joint Memorandum Circular to ensure its efficient performance in carrying the mandate of the office.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We urge Management to intensify the advertisement of vacant positions in order to generate more applicants who may be eligible for the position.

We recommended that Management advise the approving officer to stop approving loan applications of employees, the amortization of which may result in a net take home pay of less than the threshold, in compliance with the above-cited law.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We appreciate the insights provided by the management. However, a careful reading on the provision of the law, we cannot attribute any of the said insights as an exception to Section 48 of the General Provisions of the 2018 General Appropriations Act No. 10964.

We recommended that Management formulate and immediately implement a long term solution leading to a self-reliant LGU, as mandated by the Local Government Code and Section 37 of RA 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168 dated October 27, 2009, to address solid waste disposal system of the Municipality.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The construction of sanitary landfill may not only be the solution to become a self-reliant LGU in terms of solid waste disposal. If such may not be feasible, there may be other alternative remedies in order to become a self-reliant LGU.

We recommended that Management direct the  Municipal Treasurer to require the business establishments, when paying their business taxes, to submit a copy of audited financial statements or its equivalent document and strictly implement Section 171 of the Local Government Code on the  examination of Books of Accounts and pertinent records of business establishments for an accurate computation of business taxes.  It is further recommended that the LGU should maintain a database of the business establishments within its jurisdiction for effective and efficient collection of taxes.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We maintain our audit observation since Under Section 171 of RA 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code, it is clear that assessment and collection of the correct amount of tax belongs to the Local Treasurer and not to the Business Permit and Licensing Office.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort to collect the delinquent real property taxes, otherwise, apply the remedies provided under the law in order to generate more revenue that may be appropriated for the implementation of additional programs and projects for the benefit of its constituents and enforce the sanctions provided under the law for non-compliance therewith.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of Bugallon in compliance with the provisions of Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386, Section 39 of PD 1445 and Section 449 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the  Municipal Accountant maintain sub-accounts consisting of separate ledgers for the enterprise and consequently prepare trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management has incorporated this requirement in the financial statements for the year 2017. The resulting financial performance for the economic enterprises of the Municipality is included as additional schedule and also reflected in the Notes to the Financial Statements as well.
observations.reason: Management had complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the management should comply and ensure the completeness of the submission of revenue and disbursement vouchers within the time frame for timely examination and verification of accounts pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts,   Section 39 (1) and (3) of P.D 1445, Section 23 (1) and (3) of The Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that majority of the vouchers for submission were already submitted in January 2018 and the December vouchers were submitted in February 2018
observations.reason: The Team noted that the submission of vouchers together with its supporting documents were not submitted within the time frame prescribed hence reiterated in Finding No. 4 page 42 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of Perfected Contracts, bidding documents and other supporting documents to the Auditor within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 to effect timely review and audit of the contracts and related documents.
observations.managementAction: Management took into action the augmentation of manpower complement for BAC Secretariat Office to ensure timely submission of all necessary documents to the Audit Team
observations.reason: Copies of Perfected Contracts together with its supporting documents were submitted to the Team upon perfection of the contract.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management stop the payment of BAC Honoraria or Professional Services charged against SEF and Trust Fund without prejudice to the refund amounting to ₱65,083.00 contrary to Section 272 of RA 7160, Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG, Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The management has long since adhered to the audit recommendation
observations.reason: The grants of honoraria or professional fees were from the general fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to make certain that all procurements of drugs and medicines be supported with a certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products being procured are enlisted and conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition guaranteeing the quality, effectiveness and safety in catering the medical needs of its constituents and in accordance with Section 3 and Rule 36 of IRR of RA 9502.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of the Municipality were ensured to have undergone review by the Municipal Health Office to determine that items were listed in the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Volume I.
observations.reason: Management had complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned personnel to expedite execution of the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-83 dated July 18, 2014 in order that protection against hazards of disability, sickness, maternity, old age, death and other contingencies that the SSS offers could be achieved.
observations.managementAction: In the absence of an enabling ordinance from the Sanggunian Bayan, the management applied the Memorandum of Agreement between Social Security System (SSS) and the Department of Interior and Local Government.
observations.reason: Management had taken measures to let the Contractual and Job Orders have the opportunity to deduct SSS contributions.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management stop the practice of granting cash advances or reimbursement of expenses incurred by persons who are not authorized or eligible for such payments to avoid the incurrence of Irregular Expenses as defined in COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management put a hold in sending consultants to trainings and seminars.
observations.reason: No consultant was given cash advance for attending seminar-workshops.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the management observe due diligence when using Shopping as a procurement method. Also, we recommended the management designate a dedicated officer-in-charge of procuring activities to ensure effective planning and implementation of procurement activities are achieved.
observations.managementAction: Management limits the use of Shopping as a mode of procurement within the prescribed definition as provided in the provisions of R.A. 9184 otherwise known as Government Procurement Reform Act.
observations.reason: Management does not exceed in the provided threshold when shopping is used as mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to require the winning bidder to post the Performance Security before signing the contract in accordance with Section 39.1 of IRR of RA 9184. We also recommended that Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 be strictly observed in the submission of disbursements involving public bidding.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation in the succeeding purchases of motor vehicles.
observations.reason: Subsequent purchases of motor vehicles were posted with bank guarantees as performance securities.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020)  in the Trust Fund book at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Management particularly the Accounting Department complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: In the 2017 Financial Statements, management was able to transfer the unexpended balances from the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and Mitigation Fund to the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management continue to incorporate GAD issues and concerns in the GAD Plan and Budget and to optimally implement the Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) planned out in order to fulfill the roles and responsibilities LGUs have in the implementation of Magna Cart of Women in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM & NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. 

Also, consistency should be observed with the GAD activities incorporated in the Annual Budget with the GAD plan and budget in accordance with PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01, the GAD Planning and Budgeting Guidelines.
observations.managementAction:
Management is appreciative of the observation. The continued effort of the management to support GAD – related activities is rewarded through the recognition of National Government by virtue of the following recognitions:

Fully Functional Local Social Welfare and Development office
Conferred with the Seal of Child Friendly by DSWD.
observations.reason: Management has incorporated Gender and Development (GAD) issues in its plan and budget submitted to DILG.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of Bugallon in compliance with the provisions of Section 39 of PD 1445 and Section 449 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the proper handling and recording of collections and deposits in accordance with the requirements of the MNGAS for LGUs to ensure prompt reporting, remittances and deposit of collections.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the management enforce strict compliance with the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and settlement of cash advances and employ stringent measures to ensure the immediate liquidation of the cash advances as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Management should refrain in further depositing government funds in PNB & PVB both in Lingayen and transfer the remaining funds to Land Bank of the Philippines or Development Bank of the Philippines before the extended period of June 30, 2017 expires in compliance with DOF Circular No. 002-2016 dated July 8, 2016

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to intensify/improve their collection strategies on taxes due and collectible by sending RPTOPs and notice of delinquency to taxpayers and form a committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes and
If circumstances warrant, impose the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes to satisfy taxes, penalties and costs with the following:
Seizure of personal property of the taxpayer and sale thereof at public auction;
File civil suit with the proper court; and
Levy on real property and sale of delinquent property or a usable portion thereof at public auction.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine and identify the specific nature of the unaccounted or undocumented reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the affected bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management has adjusted the various long outstanding reconciling items.
observations.reason: Adjusting entries have been made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Local Chief Executive to ensure compliance with the provisions of Section 39 of PD 1445 and Section 449 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I by documenting all parcels of Land covered by Tax Declarations for possible titling.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management shall endeavour to secure copies of documents proving ownership over parcels of land and apply for titling for those untitled lots.
observations.reason: No proof of ownership presented to the Team only Tax Declarations for some lots owned by the Municipality. Reiterated in Finding   No. 1 page 37 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to ensure compliance with the proper handling and recording of collections and deposits in accordance with the requirements of the MNGAS for LGUs to ensure prompt reporting, remittances and deposit of collections.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management will comply with the said recommendation.
observations.reason: The balance of the Cash – Local Treasury as December 31, 2016 is lower than that of last year but the balance is significantly large, an indication  that collections and deposits  are still not intact. Finding   No. 2 page 38 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with DOF Department Circular Nos. 001-2015 & 003-2015 dated June 1, 2015 & August 24, 2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management has expressed that recent pronouncements made by Secretary Dominguez of DOF indicated the dropping of the DOF Circular No. 001-2015 and reviewing the draft that will revoke the said circular.
observations.reason:
Verification from the Bank statements, the municipality has been depositing funds to the PVB account for their daily operation. 
Reiterated in Finding   No. 4 page 42 of this AAR.

The amount as of December 31, 2016 is higher than CY 2015 that when collected can be beneficial to the townspeople.
Reiterated in Finding   No. 17 page 63 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation  that the Local Chief Executive  ensure strict compliance on the granting, utilization and settlement of cash advances at the end of the year as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management has expressed that were certain circumstances that affect the timely settlement of the cash advances and that they cannot compromise completeness and correctness of the documents over timeliness.
observations.reason: A partial settlement was made by the management in the amount of P544,820.09 or 63.72% for prior years’ cash advances and P1,849,901.87 or 68.31% for the current year’s cash advances. Reiterated in Finding   No. 3 page 39 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the reciprocal account and to take effect the necessary correcting entries to adjust the balances of the accounts. Also, the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor and review the transactions to ensure accuracy in recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management has determined the necessary adjustment needed and remitted the amount at year-end but was not yet deposited in the General Fund. The discrepancy is a result of timing difference.
observations.reason: Management has determined the necessary adjustment but it was only deposited on January 2017 of which deposit slip was presented to the team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management ensure the completeness in the submission of revenue and disbursement vouchers within the time frame for timely examination and verification of accounts pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts,   Section 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management shall endeavour to determine the underlying causes of delay and to effect corrective measures towards efficient and timely submission.
observations.reason:
Submission of Vouchers and its supporting documents were submitted beyond the prescribed period. 
Reiterated in Finding   No. 6 page 45 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the updating of the assessment roll, a copy of which is to be furnished to the Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTAR or taxpayer’s index cards. 

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPTAR to be used as basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the actual collectibles from real property tax in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management has expressed that the updating of the assessment roll is still in progress and that upon full update, the concerned offices will comply with the said recommendation.
observations.reason:
Verification revealed that RPT and SET receivables was set-up based on estimates by the Municipal Assessor 
Reiterated in Finding   No. 8 page 48 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all expenses charged against the SEF are in compliance with the activities enumerated under Section 272 of R.A. 7160 as well as Department of Education, Culture and Sports, Department of Budget and Management and Department of Interior and Local Government Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management mentioned that they will comply with the recommendation made by the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
There were expenses that should not be charged in the SEF and also in the trust.
Reiterated in Finding No. 9 page 49 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned personnel to strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-83 dated July 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management has sent a letter of endorsement to the Sangguniang Bayan to authorize the LCE to enter in a Memorandum of Agreement with SSS.
observations.reason: A letter of endorsement has been sent to SB but at this time, the request is on the committee level.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management observe due diligence when using Shopping as a procurement method. Also, we have recommended that the management designate a dedicated officer-in-charge of procuring activities to ensure effective planning and implementations of procurement activities are achieved.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management commits to ensure that they prescribe with the rules and regulations of the procurement law.
observations.reason:
Based on the data submitted to the team, shopping is frequently used as mode of procurement. 
Reiterated in Finding No. 13 page 56 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality continue to incorporate GAD issues and concerns in the annual budget proposal and work and financial plans in order to eradicate gender-based inequalities and enable women and men to equally contribute and benefit from development.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management has agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
Management has incorporated Gender and Development (GAD) issues in its plan and budget submitted to DILG but the plan was not consistent with the Annual Investment Plan found in the Annual Budget and allocation of such Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) was not optimally utilized.
Reiterated in Finding No. 16 page 60 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Health Officer to coordinate with the barangay health centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the government’s goal of providing preventive care services to indigents will be fully implemented, and thus, best results could be achieved in the most opportune time.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management utilized the balance to procure rescue vehicles which was given to various barangays.
observations.reason: The unutilized balance was used in part to procure 23 rescue vehicles which were granted to various barangays of Bugallon.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Property Custodian to prepare and maintain Stock Cards, Property Cards and SSMI for proper monitoring of inventories and properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed/issued, so that reconciliation of the physical count with accounting records will be completed.

Likewise, require the Property Custodian to conduct complete annual physical count of all properties of the municipality including that of the inventory items with the presence of an Auditor and submit a report thereon not later than January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management will comply with the said recommendation.
observations.reason: A Physical Count was conducted by the Municipality without the presence of an Auditor as witness and the Reports on the Physical Count of PPE and Inventory were not submitted to the Team for verification and compliance.
